name: Sint Eustatius (Caribbean Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: the bravery of the people of Statia
the Caribbean Sea around the island
the field of the central diamond and the surrounding bands
the star above the silhouetted island
the silhouette of the island, the Quill volcano and its vegetation

description: A red field bearing in the centre a white lozenge (diamond) outlined in red and blue, charged with a green silhouette of the island of Sint Eustatius surmounted by a small golden star. Four blue triangles (one in each corner of the field, joined to the central diamond) extend through the red field along the cardinal directions.
history: Adopted on 16 November 2004. The flag's elements - the red of bravery, the blue of the sea, the silhouette of the island under the Quill volcano, and the gold star recalling the island's nickname 'the Golden Rock' - were chosen to evoke Sint Eustatius's commercial heyday in the 18th century. The flag remained in use after the island became a special municipality of the Netherlands on 10 October 2010.

features.symbolism: A red field with a central blue diamond bearing a silhouette of the island; quartered blue corners give the flag its distinctive cross-on-diamond layout.

features.role: central white diamond (lozenge) bearing the silhouette of Sint Eustatius
four blue triangles framing the central diamond, set against the red field along the cardinal directions
silhouette of the island of Sint Eustatius (St. Statia)
features.symbolism: the island itself, dominated by the Quill volcano

features.role: golden five-pointed star above the island silhouette
features.symbolism: the island's status as 'the Golden Rock' for its 18th-century role as a major Atlantic trading post
